# INSTRUCTION BLOCK 1.1 — ENEMY TELEGRAPHING SYSTEM
**Spec reference:** `NEON_DUNGEON_CLAUDE_CODE_SPEC.md § 5.1`
**Depends on:** Nothing (implement first)
**Estimated complexity:** Medium

---

## PRE-IMPLEMENTATION SCAN (do this before writing any code)

Locate and report the exact names of the following in the current source:

1. The enemy action/attack resolution function (likely named something like `resolveEnemyAction`, `processEnemyTurn`, `enemyAttack`, or similar)
2. The combat log function used to write text events to the combat display
3. The enemy object structure — confirm whether a `telegraph` property already exists
4. The floating damage/text spawn function (used for numbers and status text above sprites)
5. The main player action handler — where spell button presses are processed
6. The primary game state object name (e.g. `gameState`, `state`, `game`, etc.)

**Report all six before writing any code. If any are ambiguous, stop and ask.**

---

## STEP 1 — Add telegraph as a runtime property on enemy initialization

Find where enemies are instantiated for a room (not the static data definitions). Add `telegraph: null` as a default property on every enemy at creation time. This is runtime state only — do not modify static enemy data arrays or objects.

---

## STEP 2 — Add the Telegraph System as a new named section in the JS

Insert the following as a clearly commented block. Find a logical grouping location (near combat functions, not inside any existing function):

```javascript
// ============================================================
// TELEGRAPH SYSTEM — Phase 1.1
// ============================================================

function determineTelegraph(enemy, incomingDamage, player) {
    const damageRatio = incomingDamage / Math.max(1, player.hp);
    const isSpecial = enemy.currentAbility &&
        ['aoe', 'execute', 'multi_hit', 'special'].includes(enemy.currentAbility.subtype);
    const isBoss = enemy.tier === 'boss';

    if (isBoss) return true;
    if (isSpecial) return true;
    if (damageRatio > 0.40) return true;
    if (damageRatio > 0.20) return Math.random() < 0.5;
    return false;
}

function buildTelegraph(enemy, incomingDamage, player) {
    const isBoss = enemy.tier === 'boss';

    const abilitySubtype = enemy.currentAbility?.subtype || 'physical';
    const counterMap = {
        'heavy_physical': 'shield',
        'aoe_physical':   'shield',
        'heavy_magical':  'stun',
        'execute':        'stun',
        'multi_hit':      'dodge',
        'sweep':          'dodge',
        'magical':        'reflect',
        'beam':           'reflect',
        'physical':       'shield'
    };
    const counterType = counterMap[abilitySubtype] || 'shield';

    const ratio = incomingDamage / Math.max(1, player.hp);
    let threatLevel = 'LOW';
    if (ratio >= 0.75) threatLevel = 'LETHAL';
    else if (ratio >= 0.50) threatLevel = 'HIGH';
    else if (ratio >= 0.25) threatLevel = 'MEDIUM';

    const attackName = enemy.currentAbility?.name || `${enemy.name} STRIKE`;

    return {
        active: true,
        attackName: attackName,
        attackType: abilitySubtype,
        turnsRemaining: isBoss ? 2 : 1,
        counterType: counterType,
        warningText: `\u26A1 ${enemy.name}: CHARGING ${attackName.toUpperCase()}`,
        damage: incomingDamage,
        threatLevel: threatLevel
    };
}

function checkPlayerCounter(telegraph, playerLastAction) {
    if (!telegraph || !playerLastAction) return null;

    const counterMap = {
        'shield':  ['defense', 'shield'],
        'stun':    ['stun', 'cc'],
        'dodge':   ['dodge', 'evade'],
        'reflect': ['reflect']
    };

    const validActions = counterMap[telegraph.counterType] || [];
    const isCounter = validActions.some(tag =>
        playerLastAction.type === tag ||
        (playerLastAction.tags && playerLastAction.tags.includes(tag))
    );

    if (!isCounter) return null;

    const bonuses = {
        'shield':  { type: 'negate_and_retaliate', damageMult: 0, retaliateRatio: 0.20 },
        'stun':    { type: 'cancel_and_skip', damageMult: 0 },
        'dodge':   { type: 'evade_and_empower', damageMult: 0, nextAttackMult: 1.5 },
        'reflect': { type: 'reflect_damage', damageMult: 0, reflectMult: 1.5 }
    };

    return bonuses[telegraph.counterType] || null;
}

function applyCounterBonus(counterResult, enemy, player) {
    // NOTE: Replace spawnFloatingText with the exact floating text function name in this codebase
    switch (counterResult.type) {
        case 'negate_and_retaliate':
            const retaliationDmg = Math.floor(counterResult.retaliateRatio * enemy.atk);
            enemy.hp -= retaliationDmg;
            spawnFloatingText(`COUNTER STRIKE -${retaliationDmg}`, enemy.x, enemy.y, '#ffd700');
            break;
        case 'cancel_and_skip':
            enemy.actionCounter = 0;
            spawnFloatingText('ATTACK CANCELLED!', enemy.x, enemy.y, '#ffd700');
            break;
        case 'evade_and_empower':
            state.playerNextAttackMult = counterResult.nextAttackMult;
            spawnFloatingText('EVADED! NEXT ATK +50%', player.x, player.y, '#ffd700');
            break;
        case 'reflect_damage':
            const reflectedDmg = Math.floor(enemy.atk * counterResult.reflectMult);
            enemy.hp -= reflectedDmg;
            spawnFloatingText(`REFLECTED! -${reflectedDmg}`, enemy.x, enemy.y, '#ffd700');
            break;
    }
}

function renderTelegraphWarning(ctx, telegraph, x, y) {
    if (!telegraph || !telegraph.active) return;

    const colorMap = {
        'LETHAL': '#ff4444',
        'HIGH':   '#ff8800',
        'MEDIUM': '#ffff00',
        'LOW':    '#aaaaaa'
    };
    const color = colorMap[telegraph.threatLevel] || '#aaaaaa';

    // Blink LETHAL at ~1Hz
    const visible = telegraph.threatLevel === 'LETHAL'
        ? Math.floor(Date.now() / 500) % 2 === 0
        : true;

    if (!visible) return;

    ctx.save();
    ctx.font = '9px "Press Start 2P"';

    const warningWidth = ctx.measureText(telegraph.warningText).width + 16;
    ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(10,10,26,0.85)';
    ctx.fillRect(x - 8, y - 12, warningWidth, 36);
    ctx.strokeStyle = color;
    ctx.lineWidth = 1;
    ctx.strokeRect(x - 8, y - 12, warningWidth, 36);

    ctx.fillStyle = color;
    ctx.fillText(telegraph.warningText + ` [${telegraph.turnsRemaining}T]`, x, y);

    ctx.fillStyle = '#00ffff';
    ctx.fillText(`\u2192 COUNTER: ${telegraph.counterType.toUpperCase()}`, x, y + 16);

    ctx.restore();
}
```

**Substitutions required before saving:**
- Replace `spawnFloatingText` with the actual floating text function name you found in the pre-scan
- Replace `state` with the actual game state object name you found in the pre-scan
- Replace `enemy.actionCounter` with the actual action counter property name if it differs

---

## STEP 3 — Modify enemy action resolution function

Find the enemy action resolution function identified in the pre-scan. At the very start of that function, before any attack executes, inject the following logic:

```javascript
// --- TELEGRAPH SYSTEM INJECTION (Phase 1.1) ---
const incomingDamage = calculateDamage(enemy, player); // use existing damage calc — do not duplicate
const shouldTelegraph = determineTelegraph(enemy, incomingDamage, player);

if (shouldTelegraph && !enemy.telegraph) {
    enemy.telegraph = buildTelegraph(enemy, incomingDamage, player);
    // Replace logCombatEvent with the actual combat log function name from pre-scan
    logCombatEvent(enemy.telegraph.warningText, 'warning');
    return; // Enemy spends this turn charging, does not attack
}

if (enemy.telegraph && enemy.telegraph.active) {
    enemy.telegraph.turnsRemaining--;

    if (enemy.telegraph.turnsRemaining > 0) {
        // Boss still charging (2-turn warning)
        logCombatEvent(
            `${enemy.name}: ${enemy.telegraph.attackName} INCOMING [${enemy.telegraph.turnsRemaining}T]`,
            'warning'
        );
        return;
    }

    // Telegraph fires this turn — check for player counter
    // Replace state.playerLastAction with actual path to last action in your state object
    const counterResult = checkPlayerCounter(enemy.telegraph, state.playerLastAction);

    if (counterResult) {
        applyCounterBonus(counterResult, enemy, player);
        logCombatEvent(`PERFECT COUNTER! ${counterResult.type.replace(/_/g, ' ').toUpperCase()}`, 'counter');
        enemy.telegraph = null;
        return; // Attack negated — do not fall through to normal attack
    }

    // No counter — attack executes normally, fall through to existing attack code below
    enemy.telegraph = null;
}
// --- END TELEGRAPH INJECTION ---

// ... existing attack code continues unchanged below this point ...
```

**Important:** Do not duplicate the existing attack execution code. The injection only wraps/gates it. The existing attack logic below the injection runs unchanged when there is no counter.

```javascript
    type: spellUsed.type,         // use actual property name for spell type

    name: spellUsed.name
};
```

---

## STEP 5 — Add tags to existing spell definitions

Scan the existing 30 defensive spells and add a `tags` array property where missing. Apply these tags:

- Any spell that creates a shield or damage barrier → `tags: ['shield', 'defense']`
- Any spell that applies stun, freeze, or interrupt → `tags: ['stun', 'cc', 'defense']`
- Any spell that provides evasion, dodge, or phase → `tags: ['dodge', 'evade', 'defense']`
- Any spell that reflects damage back → `tags: ['reflect', 'defense']`
- All other defensive spells → `tags: ['defense']`
- All attack spells → `tags: ['attack']`
- All potions → `tags: ['potion']`

Do not remove or rename any existing spell properties. Only add the `tags` array.

---

Add a call to `renderTelegraphWarning` for each enemy that has an active telegraph. Position the warning above or within the combat log area — use the existing layout coordinates as reference, do not hardcode arbitrary positions.

Example pattern (adapt coordinates to match actual layout):

```javascript
enemies.forEach(enemy => {
    if (enemy.telegraph && enemy.telegraph.active) {
        renderTelegraphWarning(ctx, enemy.telegraph, combatLog.x, combatLog.y - 40);
    }
```

**Estimated complexity:** Medium-High

---

## PRE-IMPLEMENTATION SCAN

Locate and report the exact names of:

1. The boss enemy definitions — where boss data is stored (static array, object, or inline)
2. The function that checks/monitors enemy HP changes during combat
3. The main combat render loop — specifically where boss HP bar is drawn
4. Any existing phase or state tracking on boss enemies (property like `phase`, `currentPhase`, etc.)
5. The screen flash / visual effect system — how full-screen effects are currently triggered
6. The function that applies buffs to entities

**Report all six before writing any code.**

---

## STEP 1 — Add phase definitions to each boss

For each boss in the game, add a `phases` array. This is added to the static boss definition data. Use this structure exactly:

```javascript
// Add to each boss definition:
phases: [
    {
        phase: 1,
        hpThreshold: 1.0,
        aiPattern: 'aggressive',         // use the pattern name already in use for this boss
        abilities: [],                   // copy the boss's current ability list here
        buffs: [],
        visualState: 'normal',
        phaseTransitionText: null
    },
    {
        phase: 2,
        hpThreshold: 0.60,
        aiPattern: 'tactical',
        abilities: [],                   // copy phase 1 abilities PLUS add 'shield_phase' and 'emp_burst' if boss has them, or leave as phase 1 list if not
        buffs: [{ type: 'atk_up', value: 0.30, duration: -1 }],
        visualState: 'enraged',
        phaseTransitionText: 'CORE SYSTEMS OVERCLOCKING... DANGER LEVEL: CRITICAL'
    },
    {
        phase: 3,
        hpThreshold: 0.25,
        aiPattern: 'desperate',
        abilities: [],                   // phase 2 abilities PLUS add execute-tier attacks
        buffs: [
            { type: 'spd_up', value: 0.50, duration: -1 },
            { type: 'atk_up', value: 0.50, duration: -1 }
        ],
        visualState: 'overloaded',
        phaseTransitionText: 'EMERGENCY PROTOCOLS ENGAGED. EXTERMINATION MODE ACTIVE.'
    }
]
```

Also add `currentPhase: 1` as a runtime property on boss enemy initialization (same place `telegraph: null` was added in Block 1.1).

---

## STEP 2 — Add phase transition functions

Insert as a new clearly commented section near the boss/combat functions:

```javascript
// ============================================================
// BOSS PHASE SYSTEM — Phase 1.2
// ============================================================

function checkBossPhaseTransition(boss, player) {
    if (!boss.phases || boss.tier !== 'boss') return;

    const hpPercent = boss.hp / boss.maxHp;
    const nextPhase = boss.phases.find(p =>
        p.phase > boss.currentPhase && hpPercent <= p.hpThreshold
    );

    if (nextPhase) {
        triggerPhaseTransition(boss, nextPhase, player);
    }
}

function triggerPhaseTransition(boss, newPhase, player) {
    // 1. Pause combat
    // Replace 'combatState' with actual combat state variable name
    combatState = 'phase_transition';
    boss.currentPhase = newPhase.phase;
    boss.aiPattern = newPhase.aiPattern;

    // 2. Apply permanent phase buffs
    newPhase.buffs.forEach(buff => {
        applyBuff(boss, buff.type, buff.value, buff.duration);
        // Replace applyBuff with actual buff application function from pre-scan
    });

    // 3. Screen flash sequence
    triggerScreenFlash('#ffffff', 3, () => {

        // 4. Display phase transition banner
        if (newPhase.phaseTransitionText) {
            showPhaseBanner(newPhase.phaseTransitionText, newPhase.phase, () => {

                // 5. Apply visual state to boss
                boss.visualState = newPhase.visualState;

                // 6. Resume combat
                combatState = 'active';
                // Replace 'active' with the actual active combat state string used in the codebase
            });
        } else {
            boss.visualState = newPhase.visualState;
            combatState = 'active';
        }
    });
}

function triggerScreenFlash(color, frameCount, onComplete) {
    let framesLeft = frameCount;
    const originalDraw = renderCombatFrame; // Replace with actual render function name

    function flashStep() {
        if (framesLeft > 0) {
            ctx.save();
            ctx.fillStyle = color;
            ctx.globalAlpha = framesLeft / frameCount * 0.8;
            ctx.fillRect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);
            ctx.restore();
            framesLeft--;
            requestAnimationFrame(flashStep);
        } else {
            if (onComplete) onComplete();
        }
    }
    requestAnimationFrame(flashStep);
}

function showPhaseBanner(text, phase, onComplete) {
    // Render a full-width text banner for 2500ms then call onComplete
    const bannerDuration = 2500;
    const startTime = Date.now();

    function bannerStep() {
        const elapsed = Date.now() - startTime;
        if (elapsed < bannerDuration) {
            ctx.save();
            ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(10,10,26,0.9)';
            ctx.fillRect(0, canvas.height / 2 - 30, canvas.width, 60);
            ctx.strokeStyle = '#ff4444';
            ctx.lineWidth = 2;
            ctx.strokeRect(0, canvas.height / 2 - 30, canvas.width, 60);
            ctx.font = '10px "Press Start 2P"';
            ctx.fillStyle = '#ff4444';
            ctx.textAlign = 'center';
            ctx.fillText(text, canvas.width / 2, canvas.height / 2 + 4);
            ctx.textAlign = 'left'; // reset
            ctx.restore();
            requestAnimationFrame(bannerStep);
        } else {
            if (onComplete) onComplete();
        }
    }
    requestAnimationFrame(bannerStep);
}
```

## STEP 4 — Dynamic boss HP bar coloring

Find where the boss HP bar is rendered. Modify the fill color based on `boss.currentPhase`:

```javascript
// Replace hardcoded boss HP bar color with:
const bossHpColor = boss.currentPhase === 3 ? '#ff4444' :
                    boss.currentPhase === 2 ? '#ff8800' :
                    '#00ffff'; // phase 1 default cyan

// For phase 3, add pulse effect:
const pulseAlpha = boss.currentPhase === 3
    ? 0.7 + Math.sin(Date.now() / 200) * 0.3
    : 1.0;
ctx.globalAlpha = pulseAlpha;
// draw HP bar fill
ctx.globalAlpha = 1.0; // reset after
```

## STEP 5 — Shield Phase mechanic

Find the boss ability execution logic. Add handling for an ability of type `shield_phase`:

```javascript
// When boss uses an ability named or typed 'shield_phase':
if (ability.type === 'shield_phase' || ability.id === 'shield_phase') {
    boss.shieldPhase = {
        active: true,
        type: 'energy_barrier',
        requiredTag: 'armor_pierce',
        turnsRemaining: 3,
        hpImmune: true,
        shieldHp: Math.floor(boss.maxHp * 0.15), // 15% of boss max HP as shield pool
        breakText: 'BARRIER SHATTERED — SYSTEMS EXPOSED'
    };
    logCombatEvent(`${boss.name} ACTIVATES ENERGY BARRIER!`, 'warning');
    return;
}
```

In the damage application function, add shield phase interception before HP reduction:

```javascript
// Before reducing boss HP:
if (enemy.shieldPhase && enemy.shieldPhase.active) {
    const hasArmorPierce = playerSpell.tags && playerSpell.tags.includes('armor_pierce');
    if (hasArmorPierce) {
        enemy.shieldPhase.shieldHp -= damage;
        if (enemy.shieldPhase.shieldHp <= 0) {
            enemy.shieldPhase.active = false;
            enemy.shieldPhase = null;
            // Apply 1-turn stun to boss
            applyDebuff(enemy, 'stun', 1);
            logCombatEvent(boss.shieldPhase?.breakText || 'BARRIER BROKEN!', 'success');
            spawnFloatingText('SHIELD BROKEN!', enemy.x, enemy.y, '#ffd700');
        } else {
            spawnFloatingText(`SHIELD -${damage}`, enemy.x, enemy.y, '#00ffff');
        }
        return; // Damage absorbed by shield, do not reduce boss HP
    } else {
        // Attack cannot pierce shield
        spawnFloatingText('BLOCKED', enemy.x, enemy.y, '#888888');
        return;
    }
}
```

Also add `tags: ['armor_pierce']` to any spells in the existing pool that should penetrate armor (heavy attack spells, energy/tech weapon attacks, overload-type spells). Use judgment on 3–5 existing spells that thematically fit.

# INSTRUCTION BLOCK 1.3 — ACTIVE DODGE WINDOW
**Spec reference:** `NEON_DUNGEON_CLAUDE_CODE_SPEC.md § 5.3`
**Depends on:** Blocks 1.1 and 1.2 must be complete
**Estimated complexity:** Medium

---

## PRE-IMPLEMENTATION SCAN

Locate and report the exact names of:

1. The existing passive dodge mechanic — where the dodge % chance is currently calculated and applied
2. The passive skill definitions — specifically the dodge passive skill entry
3. The combat input handler — where the player clicks/presses buttons during combat
4. Any existing `combatMode` setting or similar accessibility flag (it may not exist yet)
5. The canvas dimensions currently used (confirm `canvas.width` / `canvas.height` or hardcoded values)

**Report all five before writing any code.**

---

## STEP 1 — Add combatMode setting

Add a `combatMode` property to the game settings or options object (wherever settings like audio on/off are stored). Default to `'active'`:

```javascript
settings.combatMode = settings.combatMode || 'active';
// 'active' = dodge window mechanic (new)
// 'passive' = original dodge % chance (preserved for accessibility)
```

If no settings object exists, add it to the main game state object.

---

## STEP 2 — Add dodge window state to combat state

Add the following to the combat state object (wherever per-combat state is tracked):

```javascript
dodgeWindow: {
    active: false,
    indicatorPosition: 0,     // 0.0 to 1.0 (left to right)
    indicatorSpeed: 0,        // units per ms
    safeZoneStart: 0,         // 0.0 to 1.0
    safeZoneWidth: 0,         // 0.0 to 1.0
    resolved: false,
    result: null,             // 'success' | 'failure' | null
    onResolve: null           // callback function
}
```

---

## STEP 3 — Add dodge window system functions

Insert as a new clearly commented section:

```javascript
// ============================================================
// ACTIVE DODGE WINDOW SYSTEM — Phase 1.3
// ============================================================

const DODGE_BAR = {
    x: null,          // calculated at render time: canvas.width / 2 - 150
    y: null,          // calculated at render time: canvas.height - 80
    width: 300,
    height: 20
};

function openDodgeWindow(enemy, player, onResolve) {
    // If passive mode, skip window and resolve via old dodge chance
    if (settings.combatMode === 'passive') {
        const dodged = Math.random() < (player.dodgeChance || 0.05);
        onResolve(dodged ? 'success' : 'failure');
        return;
    }

    // Calculate safe zone width based on player agility stat
    // Replace player.agi with the actual agility/dexterity stat name in the codebase
    const agiStat = player.agi || player.dex || player.spd || 50; // fallback to SPD if no dedicated stat
    const safeWidthPx = Math.min(100, Math.max(20, (agiStat / 100) * 80 + 20));
    const safeZoneWidth = safeWidthPx / DODGE_BAR.width;
    const safeZoneStart = Math.random() * (1 - safeZoneWidth);

    // Speed: faster enemies = faster indicator
    const speedFactor = (enemy.spd / 100) * 2 + 0.5; // seconds to cross full bar
    const indicatorSpeed = 1 / (speedFactor * 1000); // fraction per ms

    combat.dodgeWindow = {
        active: true,
        indicatorPosition: 0,
        indicatorSpeed: indicatorSpeed,
        safeZoneStart: safeZoneStart,
        safeZoneWidth: safeZoneWidth,
        resolved: false,
        result: null,
        onResolve: onResolve,
        startTime: Date.now()
    };
}

function updateDodgeWindow(deltaTime) {
    const dw = combat.dodgeWindow;
    if (!dw.active || dw.resolved) return;

    dw.indicatorPosition += dw.indicatorSpeed * deltaTime;

    // Auto-fail if indicator passes end of bar without click
    if (dw.indicatorPosition >= 1.0) {
        dw.resolved = true;
        dw.result = 'failure';
        dw.active = false;
        dw.onResolve('failure');
    }
}

function renderDodgeWindow(ctx) {
    const dw = combat.dodgeWindow;
    if (!dw || !dw.active || dw.resolved) return;

    const bx = canvas.width / 2 - DODGE_BAR.width / 2;
    const by = canvas.height - 80;

    ctx.save();

    // Bar background
    ctx.fillStyle = '#1a1a3a';
    ctx.fillRect(bx, by, DODGE_BAR.width, DODGE_BAR.height);

    // Bar border
    ctx.strokeStyle = '#00ffff';
    ctx.lineWidth = 1;
    ctx.strokeRect(bx, by, DODGE_BAR.width, DODGE_BAR.height);

    // Safe zone
    const szX = bx + dw.safeZoneStart * DODGE_BAR.width;
    const szW = dw.safeZoneWidth * DODGE_BAR.width;
    ctx.fillStyle = '#00ff41';
    ctx.fillRect(szX, by, szW, DODGE_BAR.height);

    // Moving indicator
    const indX = bx + dw.indicatorPosition * DODGE_BAR.width - 2;
    ctx.fillStyle = '#ff00ff';
    ctx.fillRect(indX, by - 2, 4, DODGE_BAR.height + 4);

    // Label
    ctx.font = '7px "Press Start 2P"';
    ctx.fillStyle = '#00ffff';
    ctx.textAlign = 'center';
    ctx.fillText('DODGE WINDOW', canvas.width / 2, by - 8);
    ctx.textAlign = 'left';

    ctx.restore();
}

function handleDodgeWindowClick(mouseX, mouseY) {
    const dw = combat.dodgeWindow;
    if (!dw || !dw.active || dw.resolved) return false;

    // Accept click anywhere — we test whether indicator is in safe zone at click time
    const inSafeZone = dw.indicatorPosition >= dw.safeZoneStart &&
                       dw.indicatorPosition <= (dw.safeZoneStart + dw.safeZoneWidth);

    dw.resolved = true;
    dw.result = inSafeZone ? 'success' : 'failure';
    dw.active = false;

    if (inSafeZone) {
        spawnFloatingText('DODGED!', player.x, player.y, '#00ff41');
        // Replace spawnFloatingText and player.x/player.y with actual names
    } else {
        spawnFloatingText('MISS', player.x, player.y, '#ff4444');
    }

    dw.onResolve(dw.result);
    return true;
}
```

## STEP 4 — Integrate dodge window into existing dodge/attack resolution

Find where the existing passive dodge chance is currently evaluated (found in pre-scan Step 1). Replace that logic with a conditional branch:

```javascript
// REPLACE existing passive dodge calculation with:
if (settings.combatMode === 'active') {
    // Open dodge window — pause until player clicks or bar expires
    openDodgeWindow(attackingEnemy, player, (result) => {
        if (result === 'success') {
            // Dodge succeeded — skip damage application
            logCombatEvent(`${player.name} DODGED ${attackingEnemy.name}'s attack!`, 'success');
        } else {
            // Dodge failed — apply damage as normal
            applyDamageToPlayer(damage, attackingEnemy);
            // Replace applyDamageToPlayer with actual damage function
        }
    });
} else {
    // Passive mode: use original dodge chance calculation (preserve existing code here)
    // [leave original passive dodge code here — do not delete it]
}
```

```javascript
    updateDodgeWindow(deltaTime);
}
```

In the combat render pass, after drawing combat UI but before presenting the frame, add:

```javascript
renderDodgeWindow(ctx);
```

## STEP 6 — Hook dodge window click into the input handler

Find where canvas click events are processed during combat. Add dodge window handling as a priority check at the top of the combat click handler:

```javascript
// At the TOP of the combat mouse/click handler:
if (handleDodgeWindowClick(event.clientX - canvasRect.left, event.clientY - canvasRect.top)) {
    return; // Click consumed by dodge window — do not process as spell button click
}
```

Replace `canvasRect` with the actual canvas bounding rect variable used in the existing click handler.

## STEP 7 — Update passive dodge skill to modify active window

Find the existing dodge passive skill definition and add modifier properties:

```javascript
// Add to existing dodge passive skill definition:
activeModifiers: {
    safeZoneBonus: [0, 20, 40, 60],    // pixels added to safe zone per passive level (index = level)
    speedReduction: [0, 0, 0.10, 0.20]  // indicator speed reduction per level
}
```

In `openDodgeWindow`, after calculating `safeWidthPx`, apply the passive modifier if the player has the dodge passive equipped:

```javascript
const dodgePassiveLevel = player.passives?.find(p => p.id === 'passive_dodge')?.level || 0;
// Replace with actual passive lookup method used in the codebase
if (dodgePassiveLevel > 0) {
    const mods = DODGE_PASSIVE_DEF.activeModifiers; // reference actual passive definition
    safeWidthPx += mods.safeZoneBonus[dodgePassiveLevel] || 0;
    indicatorSpeed *= (1 - (mods.speedReduction[dodgePassiveLevel] || 0));
}
```

# PHASE 1 — FINAL VERIFICATION CHECKLIST

Run this after all three blocks are complete and before informing the developer that Phase 1 is done.

```
ENEMY TELEGRAPHING (1.1)
[ ] Telegraph fires on correct trigger conditions (boss always, special abilities always, damage >40% HP always, damage 20-40% HP 50% chance, damage <20% HP never)
[ ] Counter-attack bonuses apply correctly for all 4 counter types (shield, stun, dodge, reflect)
[ ] LETHAL warnings blink at ~1Hz
[ ] Telegraph clears after the telegraphed attack resolves
[ ] Existing auto-attack, spell casting, buff/debuff, and combo systems still function correctly

BOSS PHASES (1.2)
[ ] Phase 2 triggers at exactly 60% boss HP
[ ] Phase 3 triggers at exactly 25% boss HP
[ ] Phase transition pauses combat during banner display
[ ] Boss HP bar changes color at each phase (cyan → orange → pulsing red)
[ ] Phase buffs (ATK up, SPD up) apply and persist for the remainder of the fight
[ ] Shield phase blocks damage from non-piercing spells
[ ] Armor-pierce spells damage shield HP pool correctly
[ ] Shield break stuns boss for 1 turn
[ ] No existing boss fights break or crash

ACTIVE DODGE (1.3)
[ ] Dodge window renders correctly at bottom of combat area
[ ] Indicator moves left to right at correct speed
[ ] Safe zone width scales with player agility/speed stat
[ ] Clicking in safe zone = dodge success
[ ] Clicking outside safe zone = dodge failure = full damage
[ ] Bar expiring without click = dodge failure = full damage
[ ] Dodge passive increases safe zone size correctly
[ ] Passive mode (combatMode = 'passive') reverts to original behavior
[ ] Click on dodge window does not also trigger spell button below it
```

**When all boxes are checked, Phase 1 is complete. Report back to the developer.**
